concept:
Tested 3 2006/03/09 20:31 The first-line pharmacotherapy for depression is: Selective Serotonin reuptake inhibitors (SSRIs)
Antidepressants associated with hypertensive crisis are: MAOIs
Preferred antidepressant for patients with comorbid medical conditions is: Citalopram (few drug interactions)
Benzodiazepines to use in patients with liver dysfunction are: (non-P450 metabolism):
Lorazepam
Oxyzepam
Temazepam
A 17 year-old female has left arm paralysis after her boyfriend dies in a car crash. No medical cause is found, indicating: Conversion disorder
Name the defense mechanism: A mother who is angry at her husband yells at her child: Displacement
Name the defense mechanism: A pedophile enters a monastary: Reaction formation
Name the defense mechanism: A woman calmly discribes a grisly murder: Isolation
Name the defense mechanism: A hospitalized 10 year-old begins to wet his bed: Regression
A 35 year-old male has recurrent episodes of
palpitations
worsening grades
flattened affect
concrete thinking indicating: Schizophreniform disorder (diagnosis of schizophrenia requires more than 6 months of symptoms)
Key side effects of atypical antipsychotics are:
Weight gain
Type 2 DM
QT prolongation
A young weight lifter receives IV haloperidol and complains that his eyes are deviated sideways. Diagnosis? Treatment?
Diagnosis: Acute dystonia (oculogyric crisis)
Treatment: Benztropine or diphenhydramine
Medication to avoid in patients with a history of alcohol withdrawl seizures is: Neuroleptics
A 13 year-old male has a history of:
theft
vandalism
violence towards family pets indicating: Conduct disorder
Patient:
is five-month-old
is female
has decreasing head growth
has truncal dyscoordination
has decreased social interaction indicating: Rett's disorder
Common therapy for phobias, obsessive-compulsive disorder (OCD) and panic disorder is: Cognitive behavioral therapy (CBT)
A patient hasn't slept for days, lost $20,000 gambling, is agitated, and has pressured speech. Diagnosis? Treatment?
Diagnosis: Acute mania
Treatment: Start a mood stabilizer (e.g. lithium)
After a minor fender bender, a man
wears a neck brace
requests permanent disability indicating: Malingering
A nurse
with severe hypoglycemia
with blood analysis showing no elevation in C-Peptide indicating: Factitious disorder (Munchausen syndrome)
A patient continues to use cocaine after being in jail, losing his job and not paying child support, indicating: Substance abuse
A violent patient has vertical and horizontal nystagmus indicating: Phencyclidine hydrochloride (PCP) intoxication
A woman who was abused as a child frequently feels outside of or detached from her body indicating: Depersonalization disorder
A man has repeated, intense urges to rub his body against unsuspecting passengers on a bus indicating: Frotteurism (a paraphilia)
A schizophrenic patient takes haloperidol for one year and develops uncontrollable tongue movements. Diagnosis? Treatment?
Diagnosis: Tardive dyskinesia
Treatment: Decrease or discontinue haloperidol and consider other antipsychotic (e.g. risperidone, clozapine)
A man unexpectedly flies across the country, takes a new name and has no memory of his prior life indicating: Dissociative fugue
